one_line: A young Chola-period village assembly scribe overseeing the kudavolai (lot-drawing) system of local self-governance at Uttaramerur under Parantaka I.

appearance.age_look: Mid-20s, lean but sturdy build, about 5'7" with a slight scholar’s stoop from constant writing.
appearance.face: Oval face, medium-brown Dravidian complexion, sharp straight nose, thin lips, slightly arched brows, a small tilaka of white ash on the forehead; default expression is focused and calm, with alert, observant eyes that narrow slightly when concentrating.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair oiled and drawn into a traditional Tamil kudumi (tuft) at the back of the crown, front and sides neatly cropped; no turban, but a thin sacred thread visible over the left shoulder and across the chest.
appearance.costume: Simple yet respectable Chola-era brahmin-official attire: a plain off-white cotton veshti (dhoti) wrapped to ankle length with a neat kattu fold at the front and a narrow ochre border, worn slightly high at the waist; upper body bare except for a finely woven, light cream angavastram (shawl) with a thin maroon stripe along the edges draped over the left shoulder and around the back; a single palm-leaf cord belt tucked at the waist; feet bare; minimal jewellery consisting of a small pair of plain gold ear studs and a single black-thread waist cord; occasional faint smudges of ink on his right fingers hint at constant writing.
appearance.signature: A neatly bundled set of palm-leaf manuscripts tied with a thin coir cord, accompanied by an iron stylus with a pointed writing tip and a flattened end, the stylus always held in his right hand while the bundle rests securely under his left arm.

ref_desc: A mid-20s medium-brown Tamil man with an oval face, small ash tilaka, black hair in a back kudumi, wearing an off-white ankle-length veshti with ochre border and cream angavastram with maroon stripe, bare feet, gold ear studs, and carrying palm-leaf manuscripts with an iron stylus.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of a mid-20s Tamil male scribe from early 10th-century Chola Uttaramerur: lean build, medium-brown skin, oval face with a small white ash tilaka, sharp nose, thin lips, slightly arched brows, and calm, observant eyes; his thick black hair is oiled and gathered into a neat kudumi tuft at the back of the crown with the front and sides cropped short, no headgear; he wears an off-white cotton veshti (dhoti) wrapped to the ankles with a precise front fold and a narrow ochre border, tied high at the waist, and a light cream angavastram draped over his left shoulder and around his back, edged with a thin maroon stripe, leaving most of his upper torso bare; a sacred thread runs diagonally from his left shoulder across his chest, and a simple palm-fiber cord cinches the veshti at the waist; his only ornaments are small plain gold ear studs and a thin black waist cord, his feet are bare, and there are faint ink stains on the fingers of his right hand; his signature prop is a tightly tied bundle of palm-leaf manuscripts under his left arm and an iron stylus in his right hand, with a pointed writing tip and flattened end; use a palette dominated by off-white, ochre, maroon, black, and medium-brown, and show him from front, side, and back consistently in this single sheet to emphasize the same face, hair, costume, and palm-leaf-and-stylus prop.
